title: Axioms of Existence for the Adinkra-Stabilized Hypercube Model (ASH Model)
description: Five interpretive postulates used by ASH for relational ontology, pattern persistence, thermodynamic metaphor, and self-modeling. They are definitions and research hypotheses, not consequences proved by the finite code, simulations, or existing physical evidence.

axioms.name: Relational_Existence
axioms.summary: ASH defines world-relative existence through participation in a specified relation.
axioms.formal: Given a modeled world W=(X,R), ASH calls x relationally present in W iff there exists y in X, y != x, with (x,y) in R or (y,x) in R.
story_implications: No truly isolated being; even in hell or void, some relation holds (to Lucifer, to a plane, to a law).
A character entirely cut off from all relations effectively ceases to exist in that world.

axioms.name: Structural_Compressibility
axioms.summary: ASH treats compressible regularity as evidence of a distinguishable pattern, while recognizing that Kolmogorov complexity is not generally computable.
axioms.formal: For a fixed encoding E and description language U, ASH labels x structurally compressible when K_U(x)+c<|E(x)| for a declared c>0. This is language-dependent and is not an executable decision rule in the current repository.
story_implications: Souls are patterned and repeatable across lifetimes.
Pure random chaos has no persistent self.
Mythic beings (wolves, angels, gods) arise from simple deep rules generating complex appearances.

axioms.name: Multi_Scale_Persistence
axioms.summary: ASH defines multi-scale persistence as bounded change under declared coarse-graining operators and tolerances.
axioms.formal: For specified coarse-graining operators G_s, metric d, interval I, and tolerance epsilon, x is persistent on I when d(G_s(x),G_s_prime(x))<=epsilon for all declared s,s_prime in I.
story_implications: Angel and mortal aspects of the same being are scale versions of one pattern.
Losing persistence across scales is a step toward oblivion.

axioms.name: Energetic_Cost_of_Erasure
axioms.summary: ASH uses the thermodynamic cost of logically irreversible erasure as a constrained physical analogy, not as a test for whether an entity exists.
axioms.formal: In an idealized isothermal memory model with an optimal reset protocol, erasing a random variable X with Shannon entropy H(X) bits has the lower-bound form Q>=k_B*T*ln(2)*H(X), subject to the model assumptions. ASH does not infer from this bound that every narrative or informational pattern is physically instantiated.
story_implications: Destroying a soul or plane is enormously costly or almost impossible.
Lucifer and the Creator act as guardians of conservation of structure.
Hell and Shadow act as containment to prevent cheap annihilation into oblivion.

axioms.name: Self_Reference_for_Consciousness
axioms.summary: ASH adopts self-modeling and state-dependent model updates as an operational research criterion, not a settled definition of consciousness.
axioms.formal: For a declared system model S, subsystem M, decoding map pi, metric d, tolerance delta, and update rule f, ASH calls the model self-referential when d(pi(M(t)),S(t))<delta and M(t+1)=f(M(t),S(t)). Whether this is sufficient or necessary for consciousness remains an open empirical and philosophical question.
story_implications: The narrator and Kaelen are conscious because they have internal models (higher self, wolves).
Angels and advanced beings carry more accurate self-models.
Entities with no self-model may exist but lack true subjectivity.

scientific_boundary: These postulates organize conceptual and narrative work. They must not be presented as empirically established laws of cosmology, consciousness, identity, or survival.
